qa_pairs.question: What is a recommended alternative for anonymous FTP with read-only access?
qa_pairs.answer: For anonymous FTP with read-only access, you should use HTTP.

qa_pairs.question: What are the security concerns with anonymous FTP for read/write access?
qa_pairs.answer: The security risks of accepting anonymous incoming file transfers for read/write access are very great. It is recommended to contact technical support for a secure solution.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I need anonymous FTP with read/write access?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need anonymous FTP with read/write access, you should contact technical support and describe your use case so they can help you find a secure solution, due to the very great security risks involved.

qa_pairs.question: What is a safer and easier alternative to authenticated FTP?
qa_pairs.answer: A safer and easier alternative to authenticated FTP (requiring usernames and passwords) is SFTP.

qa_pairs.question: What is FTPS and how does it secure data?
qa_pairs.answer: FTPS is an extension of FTP that uses TLS (Transport Layer Security) to encrypt data sent and received, providing an alternative for authenticated FTP users.

qa_pairs.question: Why should data be encrypted when authenticating FTP users with passwords?
qa_pairs.answer: When authenticating users via passwords, the transmitted data should be encrypted to prevent an eavesdropper from discovering the password.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it strongly recommended not to allow password logins to a VM?
qa_pairs.answer: It is strongly recommended not to allow password logins to your VM because automated brute-force attempts to crack passwords can be expected on any machine connected to the internet.

qa_pairs.question: What authentication method is recommended instead of password logins for VMs?
qa_pairs.answer: Instead of password logins for VMs, ssh-key authentication is recommended.

qa_pairs.question: Can SFTP be configured to use ssh-key authentication?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, SFTP can be configured to use ssh-key authentication.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find guides for setting up FTP if I cannot choose an alternative?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not have the freedom to choose an alternative to FTP, you can refer to the Ubuntu guide at https://help.ubuntu.com/lts/serverguide/ftp-server.html or the CentOS 6 guide at https://www.digitalocean.com/community/tutorials/how-to-set-up-vsftpd-on-centos-6--2.

qa_pairs.question: What must be done with FTP ports on my VM to allow access?
qa_pairs.answer: The ports that FTP uses must be open on your VM. You should consult the 'Managing your cloud resources with OpenStack#Security Groups' page for information about opening ports.

qa_pairs.question: Which port does FTP use to initiate file transfer requests?
qa_pairs.answer: FTP uses port 21 to initiate file transfer requests.

qa_pairs.question: On which ports can the actual FTP file transfer take place?
qa_pairs.answer: The actual FTP file transfer can take place on a randomly chosen port above port 1025. Port 20 can also be involved, though the exact details vary depending on the FTP mode.

qa_pairs.question: Which ports need to be opened on a VM to allow FTP access?
qa_pairs.answer: To allow FTP access on your VM, you must open port 21, possibly port 20, and probably ports 1025 and above.

qa_pairs.question: Why are other protocols generally preferred over FTP?
qa_pairs.answer: Other protocols are preferred to FTP because every open port represents a security risk, and FTP requires multiple ports to be open.
